meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master AI Portfolio Building: Debug Code Faster with Claude
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how advanced portfolio building using Antigravity and Claude Code can skyrocket your freelance efficiency and output.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop staring at the monitor. Shift your focus to building modular, portfolio-ready components. By utilizing Antigravity to structure your architecture and Claude Code to iterate on logic, you transform a debugging nightmare into a high-value asset that showcases your technical expertise to potential clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of cryptic stack traces, the deadline is breathing down your neck, and your client is expecting a polished, bug-free deployment. This is the 'debugging trap'—where you spend more time fixing ghosts than building value. It’s not just frustrating; it’s a direct hit to your billable hours and professional reputation.

But what if you stopped fighting the code and started building the solution? By shifting your workflow toward an 'AI-first' portfolio-building methodology, you turn every bug into a refined, reusable module. This isn't just about fixing errors; it’s about architecting a system that works, iterates, and sells your skills for you.

steps.title: Isolate the Architectural Failure
steps.description: Identify the specific module causing the bottleneck. We need to decouple the buggy logic from the core application to ensure we aren't chasing shadows in the main codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached codebase and identify the specific module causing the runtime error. Provide a dependency graph and suggest a decoupling strategy to isolate the logic.

steps.title: Refactor into a Reusable Component
steps.description: Transform the buggy logic into a clean, well-documented component that can be added to your public portfolio. This makes the code robust and marketable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic into a clean, production-ready component. Include JSDoc comments, unit tests, and a README snippet explaining the technical challenge and solution.

steps.title: Automate Test-Driven Debugging
steps.description: Stop debugging manually. Use Claude Code to write comprehensive test suites that validate the component's edge cases and ensure it never breaks again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive Jest test suite for this component, covering edge cases, async error handling, and performance benchmarks.

steps.title: Document for Clients and Peers
steps.description: Professional code requires professional documentation. Create a technical showcase that explains the 'why' behind your implementation choices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 'Case Study' README for this component. Highlight the technical problem, the refactoring approach, and the performance metrics achieved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --doc --file ./README.md --topic 'Technical Case Study: Optimized Component Logic'

steps.title: Integrate and Validate
steps.description: Finalize the integration by swapping the old buggy logic with your new, verified component and running a final regression check.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the integration of the new component into the main codebase. Check for potential side effects and performance regressions.

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'Sandbox' folder in your repo to iterate on complex logic independently of your production build.
Use Claude Code to generate 'ADRs' (Architecture Decision Records) as you build; it makes you look like a Principal Engineer to clients.
Never fix a bug without writing a test case for it; this turns a 'fix' into a 'feature' for your portfolio.
Tag your AI-refactored commits with #RefactoredByAI to track how much time you save over a month of client work.
When a bug seems impossible, ask the AI to 'explain the failure in the context of a junior developer' to find simple logic gaps you might have overlooked.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully refactored your first component, push it to a public GitHub repository. Use the generated documentation to build a 'Case Study' page on your portfolio site to demonstrate your problem-solving process to high-paying clients.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your freelance career. Build assets that prove your worth and reclaim your development time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
